title: Columnea pendula
taxonomy: Columnea pendula is classified as a species of flowering plant in the family Gesneriaceae. This family consists of over 1,400 species, commonly known as the African violet or Gesneriad family. The genus Columnea contains approximately 150 species, which are primarily native to the tropical regions of Central and South America. Columnea pendula is a type of epiphytic plant, meaning it grows on other plants without deriving nutrients from them. The plant is also known to be a member of the order Lamiales.

intro: Columnea pendula, also known as the 'Hanging Columnea', is a species of flowering plant in the family Gesneriaceae, native to the tropical regions of Central and South America. Traditionally, this plant has been used in folk medicine to treat various health conditions, including fever, rheumatism, and respiratory issues, due to its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. The bioactive constituents of Columnea pendula include alkaloids, flavonoids, and phenolic acids, which contribute to its medicinal properties. The most common herbal preparations made with Columnea pendula are infusions, decoctions, and tinctures, which are used to treat a range of health conditions, including digestive issues and skin problems.
